data.title: Torex Gold resumes operations at ELG underground mine in Mexico
data.description:
Torex Gold Resources (OTCPK:TORXF) +6% in Monday's trading after saying it resumed operations at the El Limón Guajes underground mine in Mexico, where three workers died from carbon monoxide exposure.

Torex (OTCPK:TORXF) said it also resumed underground development and construction activities at Media Luna, a nearby project that is approaching conclusion.

FY 2024 production guidance and the schedule for the Media Luna project were not affected by the incident, the company said.

Torex (OTCPK:TORXF) said it has begun an investigation to determine the cause of the accident, but early indications are that the carbon monoxide exposure was an isolated incident in an area of the mine that was being prepared for ventilation following a stope blast.

The new Media Luna project will be tied into the El Limón Guajes complex, and combined are expected to produce 450K-470K oz of gold this year.

data.title: Google rises as tech giant unveils 'mind-boggling' quantum computing chip
data.description:
• Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) shares rose on Monday as the tech giant unveiled what it has called a "mind-boggling" quantum computing chip.
  • The chip, known as Willow, is capable of performing calculations in five minutes that would take the world’s most powerful supercomputers 10 septillion years, the tech giant said in a study published in Nature. "Our results present device performance that, if scaled, could realize the operational requirements of large scale fault-tolerant quantum algorithms," Google researchers wrote in the study's abstract.
  • Quantum computing stocks were mixed on Monday following the news, as Rigetti Computing (RGTI) traded higher by more than 2%. Conversely, IonQ (IONQ), Qubit Computing (QUBT) and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) were sharply lower in late morning trading.

data.title: Barnes & Noble Education rallies after management points to a narrowed focus
data.description:
Barnes & Noble Education (NYSE:BNED) reported revenue fell 1.4% year-over-year in FQ2 to $602.1 million. The revenue decline was primarily driven by a net decrease of 109 physical and virtual locations, many of which were closures of the company's underperforming stores, which has helped to improve overall profitability.

"Strong adoption of our First Day affordable access programs, exceptional retail execution supporting our client institutions, and a disciplined focus on operational efficiency are reflected in our outstanding results," stated CEO Jonathan Shar. He said the company is confident in its improving momentum and the opportunities ahead.

Looking ahead, Barnes & Noble Education (BNED) management is working on simplifying operations to better focus on its core physical and virtual bookstore businesses. Notably, go-forward savings from recently completed and in-progress initiatives are now estimated at over $20 million.

As for strategy, the company's budget goals continue to target a material improvement in fiscal year 2025 GAAP operating results and adjusted EBITDA versus last year. Based on current estimates from management of capital expenditures and significantly reduced interest costs from last fiscal year, BNED believes it can drive meaningful operating free cash flow, which will be used to further de-lever its balance sheet.

Shares of Barnes & Noble Education (BNED) rallied 6.2% in late Monday morning trading. The consumer discretionary stock is still down more than 90% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: TDb Split class A share consolidation
data.description:
• TDb Split (TSX:XTD:CA) has announced a Class A share consolidation, where the recent extension of the Co.'s termination date included a retraction right for Class A shareholders and Preferred shareholders, which resulted in more Preferred shares being tendered than Class A shares.
  • To restore balance in the number of shares outstanding for each class of shares, Class A shareholders will receive 0.50 Class A shares for each Class A share they hold.
  • The total intrinsic value of Class A shares will remain the same, but the net asset value per share will increase proportionally following the consolidation.
  • This adjustment will also result in a higher asset coverage ratio for Preferred shares.
  • The impact of the Class A share consolidation will be reflected in the next reported net asset value per unit as at Dec. 13, 2024.

data.title: AbbVie reports Phase 3 data for tavapadon in Parkinson's disease
data.description:
• AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) reported positive topline results from a Phase 3 study of its drug tavapadon in the treatment of early Parkinson’s disease.
  • The study, which evaluated tavapadon as a flexible-dose monotherapy, met its primary endpoint of showing statistically significant improvement from baseline compared to placebo in the Movement Disorder Society - Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale, or MDS-UPDRS, Parts II and III combined score at week 26.
  • The trial also met a key secondary endpoint of demonstrating a statistically significant and clinically meaningful improvement in motor aspects of experiences of daily living in the tavapadon group compared to placebo at week 26, according to a statement.
  • AbbVie (ABBV) said it was on track to submit a New Drug Application for the drug to the FDA in 2025.

data.title: SAP slashes its German workforce by 3,500: report
data.description:
SAP (NYSE:SAP), one of the world's largest providers of enterprise software, is expected to reduce its workforce in Germany by about 3,500, according to a report today by Handelsblatt.

SAP has about 25,000 employees based in Germany. Employees have until Tuesday to sign up for an early retirement and/or severance pay program. The report said approximately 3,500 employees are expected to sign a termination agreement, citing sources familiar with the situation.

The job cuts are part of a wider restructuring plan announced in January of this year, which was expected to target a total of approximately 8,000 employees. The plan focuses on growth in the company's artificial intelligence offerings.

"We now estimate that between 9,000 and 10,000 positions will be affected with the corresponding impact on restructuring provisions, cash out and run rate savings after completion of the program," said SAP Chief Financial Officer Dominik Asam, during a July earnings call.

"And now you'll see the hiring actually accelerate because these kind of initiatives to hire are a little bit more back-end loaded, they'll take some time," said SAP CEO Christian Klein, during the call. "You might have noticed that while we increase the reductions by 1,000 to 2,000 and positions being affected, we are still keeping the target for the full-year in constant. So that means there is a significant ramp-up in headcount."

SAP shares were static during Monday morning trading, but they have increased more than 60% year to date.

data.title: Chinese fintech stocks surge after China signals looser monetary policy
data.description:
UP Fintech (NASDAQ:TIGR), Futu Holdings (NASDAQ:FUTU), KE Holdings (NYSE:BEKE), and Lufax Holding (NYSE:LU) were among Chinese stock trading and real estate app stocks that surged after China said it will adopt "appropriately loose" monetary policy next year. Such a move would represent the country's first policy easing in 14 years, combined with a fiscal policy to boost economic growth, according to media reports.

China will increase use of "unconventional" countercyclical adjustments that aim to expand domestic demand, Reuters said on Monday, citing Xinhua's reporting on a meeting of top Communist Party officials.

"A more proactive fiscal policy and an appropriately loose monetary policy should be implemented, enhancing and refining the policy toolkit, strengthening extraordinary counter-cyclical adjustments," the readout of the meeting said.

Easier fiscal and monetary policy bodes well for stock trading and real estate transactions as it becomes cheaper to borrow money.

In another favorable development for the world's second-largest economy, China's consumer price index rose only 0.2% Y/Y in November, easing from 0.3% in October and lower than market expectations for a 0.5% increase.

KraneShares CSI China Internet ETF (NYSEARCA:KWEB) moved up 11% in late Monday morning trading in U.S. markets. UP Fintech Holding (NASDAQ:TIGR) class A ordinary shares soared 34%, Futu Holdings (NASDAQ:FUTU) American depositary shares surged 22%, Ke Holdings (NYSE:BEKE), which operates a popular real estate platform, saw its ADSs climb 16%, ald Lufax Holding (NYSE:LU) ADSs gained 14%.

data.title: MGIC Investment cut to Underperform at BofA on premium valuation
data.description:
MGIC Investment (NYSE:MTG) shares drifted down 3.7% in Monday late morning trading after Bank of America Securities downgraded the mortgage insurer to Underperform from Buy due to its premium valuation.

The stock is changing hands at 9.2 times analyst Mihir Bhatia's 2025 earnings forecast, a premium to peers, he wrote in a note. On a price-to-book basis, (NYSE:MTG) is trading at 1.25x, also a premium to peers.

Seeking Alpha's Quant system gives (MTG) a valuation grade of C, with the poorest mark in price-to-sales and best score in price-to-earnings.

Shares gained nearly 28% this year, in line with the S&P 500's climb.

BofA's Underperform rate differs with the SA Quant rating of Strong Buy and the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.

data.title: Sibanye Stillwater to sell uranium-rich Beatrix shaft to Neo Energy in $28M deal
data.description:
Sibanye Stillwater (NYSE:SBSW) +9.4% in Monday's trading after saying it agreed to sell its Beatrix 4 shaft, which includes the Beisa uranium project, to Neo Energy Metals for 500M South African rand (~$28M) in cash and shares.

The move grants Sibanye (SBSW) a 40% equity stake in Neo Energy, positioning it as the company's largest shareholder and ensuring continued exposure to the uranium market.

Sibanye (SBSW) placed the Beatrix 4 shaft on care and maintenance in 2023 due to dwindling gold reserves and weak uranium prices, but uranium prices have since rebounded, reaching as high as $106/lb in January 2024.

Despite uranium's price recovery, Sibanye (SBSW) did not prioritize capital investment in the Beisa project, viewing the sale as a strategic opportunity to allow Neo Energy to advance the asset's development.

data.title: Consumers' inflation expectations tick up in NY Fed survey, but so does earnings outlook
data.description:
American consumers' inflation expectations ticked up against three time horizons, while their outlook for their own earnings growth over the next year increased slightly more, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of New York's November 2024 Survey of Consumer Expectations, released on Monday. In addition, for the most part, they expect to keep up with debt payments over the next three months.

Median inflation expectations rose 0.1 percentage point at all three time horizons — one year, three years, and five years. The median year-ahead inflation expectation edged up to 3.0%, three-year-ahead increased to 2.6%, and five-year ahead climbed to 2.9%. However, uncertainty over median inflation outcomes also increased across all three timeframes.

Median one-year-ahead earnings growth expectations rose 0.2 pp to 3.0% in November, still within a range of 2.7%-3.0% that the metric has kept at since January 2024.

Mean unemployment expectations — or the mean probability that the U.S. unemployment rate will be higher a year from now, rose 0.5 pp to 35.0% in November, still below its trailing 12-month average of 37.0%. Consumers were also slightly more worried about their own jobs. The mean perceived probability of losing one's job in the next 12 months increased by 0.5 pp to 13.5%.

Household income growth expectations, though, are slightly lower than the earnings growth outlook, rising by 0.1 pp to 3.1% in November. That series has been holding within a 2.9%-3.3% band since January 2023.

Median household spending growth expectations dipped 0.2 pp to 4.7%, the lowest reading since April 2021, but above prepandemic levels.

The average perceived probability of missing a minimum debt payment over the next three months dropped by 0.7 pp to 13.2%, the lowest reading since June 2024.

On Friday, the University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index rose for the fifth straight month, as consumers see now as a good time to buy durables to avoid future price increases. That survey's year-ahead inflation expectation rose to 2.9% in December from 2.6% in November.
